text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Adding salt earlier can mess with the structure of the eggs.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"The reason we don't add it earlier is because it can mess with the structure of the eggs.","entailment":"The quote explicitly states that adding salt earlier messes the egg structure, supporting the claim.","population":"eggs","intervention":"adding salt earlier","comparator":"not adding salt earlier","outcome":"messing with the structure of the eggs","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Whisking with a fork instead of a regular whisk reduces air bubbles in the omelet mixture.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"notice how I'm whisking with a fork instead of a regular whisk, because a whisk would give us too many air bubbles","entailment":"The quote explains that using a fork rather than a whisk prevents the excess air bubbles that a whisk would create, supporting the claim.","population":"omelet mixture","intervention":"whisking with a fork","comparator":"whisking with a regular whisk","outcome":"fewer air bubbles","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"The cooking process should be slow and include adding 1 or 2 small squares of butter before the egg is added.","claimType":"methodological","quote":"We want this process to be nice and slow, and then add in 1 or 2 small squares of butter, and in goes our egg, all at once working quickly similar to making those scrambled eggs.","entailment":"The quote describes a method that calls for a slow process, adding butter, then adding the egg, thereby supporting the procedural claim.","population":"","intervention":"adding 1 or 2 small squares of butter then adding the egg","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"1 or 2 small squares of butter","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Making a perfect French omelet requires a lot of patience.","claimType":"value","quote":"Making a perfect French omelet takes a lot of patience.","entailment":"The quote directly states that patience is required to make a perfect French omelet, supporting the value claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"normative"},{"statement":"If the last little bit of jiggle on the top of the omelet is fixed, the omelet is ready to be rolled.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"If we can fix that last little bit of jiggle on the top here we are ready to roll.","entailment":"The quote indicates that fixing the remaining jiggle makes the omelet ready to roll, supporting the claim about readiness after jiggle is removed.","population":"omelet","intervention":"fixing the last little bit of jiggle on the top","comparator":"","outcome":"omelet is ready to roll","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"conditional"}]}
